subject: DevOps
title: The Cost of Late Security Fixes
description: Learn The Cost of Late Security Fixes in one minute.

hook.text: Fixing a bug in design costs $1. In testing, $100. In production, $10,000. Timing is everything.

buildup.text: The later you find a flaw, the costlier the fix. Deployed code is harder to change safely.

discovery.text: Equifax's 2017 breach exploited a known flaw. A patch existed for months. Nobody applied it in time.

twist.text: Automated scans in CI/CD catch most issues before deploy. Prevention beats remediation every time.

climax.text: IBM found that orgs detecting breaches within 200 days save $1.2 million over slower responders.

punchline.text: Fix early, fix cheap. Every day a flaw lives, the cost grows.

quiz.question: Why is it more expensive to fix security issues later in the lifecycle?
options: Deployed code is harder to change and the blast radius is larger
Security tools cost more in production
